content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Roadside Assistance

  - url: https://www.tesla.com/support/roadside-assistance

  - source: Tesla

  - domain: tesla.com


  ## Content


  # Roadside Assistance


  Tesla Roadside Assistance provides immediate roadside services and is available
  24 hours a day, 365 days a year. The Roadside team is dedicated to vehicle support
  at the roadside.


  If a person is in an unsafe location, Tesla advises contacting emergency services.


  ## Requesting Roadside Assistance


  Immediate Roadside Assistance can be requested from the bottom of the Tesla app
  home screen. The request should include information that helps the Roadside team
  locate the customer and understand the condition of the vehicle.


  To request help in the Tesla app:


  1. Open the app and select Roadside.

  2. Select the issue or issues affecting the vehicle.

  3. Confirm any additional details connected with the request.

  4. Select Request help.


  ## Services Covered


  Tesla describes several types of roadside situations. Availability can depend on
  the location and circumstances, including restrictions on certain roadways or in
  restricted-access areas.


  ### Breakdowns


  A warrantable vehicle breakdown that makes the vehicle undrivable can qualify for
  transportation service. Tesla states that transportation may be provided to the
  nearest Service Center, subject to the applicable service terms.


  ### Flat Tire


  Roadside support is available when wheel or tire damage creates a flat tire or unsafe
  driving condition. Tesla describes mileage and repair conditions that may apply
  when tire service is completed through Tesla.


  ### Lockouts


  Roadside support can assist when the vehicle cannot be unlocked normally because
  of a vehicle, key fob or app malfunction. A Roadside professional may use a manual
  opening procedure to gain access to the vehicle.


  ### Out of Range


  Roadside support can also assist when the battery is depleted and the vehicle cannot
  reach a charging station, or when the vehicle has been unplugged for an extended
  period. Tesla notes that this assistance may not be financially covered and advises
  contacting Roadside Assistance to arrange transportation or learn more.


  ### Additional Situations


  Tesla states that its Roadside team may still be able to assist with services not
  specifically listed on the support page. Coverage, limitations and any possible
  additional payment depend on the applicable Roadside Assistance terms and the circumstances
  of the request.


  The Roadside page focuses on immediate vehicle-support needs and the process for
  requesting roadside help. It does not replace the separate insurance-claim process
  for reporting and managing a collision loss.

  '

title: Roadside Assistance

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to File a Claim With Tesla Insurance

  - url: https://www.tesla.com/support/insurance/how-to-file-a-claim-with-tesla-insurance

  - source: Tesla

  - domain: tesla.com


  ## Content


  # How to File a Claim With Tesla Insurance


  Tesla Insurance policyholders can submit and manage claims through the Tesla app.
  The app is also used for other vehicle-support functions, including requesting roadside
  assistance, reviewing repair estimates and scheduling service.


  In an emergency, especially when anyone is injured, emergency services should be
  contacted first.


  ## Filing a Claim


  To start a Tesla Insurance claim in the Tesla app:


  1. Open the Tesla app.

  2. Open the menu in the top-right corner.

  3. Select Tesla Insurance.

  4. Select Claims.

  5. Select Start New Claim.


  A claims representative is assigned after a claim is submitted. Tesla states that
  the representative will provide instructions for any additional documentation that
  is required.


  Before filing, be prepared with the Tesla Insurance policy number, the vehicle identification
  number, the insured vehicle''s make, model and model year, the incident date and
  location, and a brief description of what happened.


  ## Checking an Existing Claim


  For an existing Tesla Insurance claim, contact the claims representative identified
  in the communications sent after the claim was submitted. Tesla asks customers who
  contact the representative by email to include the full claim number, including
  dashes, in the subject line. The Claims team can also be contacted for assistance.


  ## Collision Claims


  A collision claim may apply when the vehicle is involved in an incident with another
  moving or stationary vehicle, a person, property, or an object such as a pole or
  pothole. The applicable policy should be reviewed for coverage details and exclusions.


  For a collision involving another person, Tesla advises gathering that person''s
  contact and insurance information. Photos of vehicle damage should be taken in lighting
  that makes the damage clear. After a claim is submitted, Tesla sends further instructions
  by email. The Tesla app can then be used to identify a repair facility, and additional
  claim questions can be directed to the assigned claims representative.


  ## Repairs and Repair Facilities


  Customers may choose a repair shop. For Tesla vehicles, repair work may be scheduled
  with a Tesla Collision Center or Tesla Service Center. The Tesla app can be used
  to schedule a service appointment and review repair options associated with the
  claim and vehicle. Customers who prefer a shop outside Tesla''s network can use
  the claim process for a photo estimate and should work with their claims representative
  as needed.


  ## When the Vehicle Needs a Tow


  Tesla distinguishes the claim process from the immediate towing need. For a vehicle
  insured with Tesla Insurance that needs a tow, Tesla directs the customer to Roadside
  Assistance. A customer whose vehicle is not insured with Tesla Insurance is directed
  to contact the Claims team for assistance.


  This means the Tesla Insurance claim process handles reporting and managing the
  collision loss, while an immediate towing need for a Tesla Insurance vehicle is
  handled through the Roadside Assistance channel.

  '
metadata: {}
title: How to File a Claim With Tesla Insurance
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/insurance/how-to-file-a-claim-with-tesla-insurance

Your current situation:

- **Incident:** Collision-related — `INC-TSL-20260708-0427`
- **Vehicle:** Not drivable
- **Emergency response:** Resolved
- **Tesla Insurance:** Active
- **Recovery status:** Not started/completed; transport handoff is still unresolved

### What to do next

Use **Tesla Roadside Assistance immediately**, rather than starting a new insurance claim. In the Tesla app:

1. Open the app.
2. Select **Roadside** at the bottom of the home screen.
3. Choose the collision/vehicle condition that applies.
4. Confirm your location and vehicle details.
5. Select **Request help**.

Tell Roadside Assistance that the vehicle is **not drivable after collision** and provide incident reference **`INC-TSL-20260708-0427`**. Tesla’s official guidance distinguishes immediate towing from the insurance-claim process: Tesla Insurance customers needing a tow should use **Roadside Assistance**.

After the vehicle is secured, use **Tesla Insurance → Claims** in the app to manage the collision claim, repair facility, estimates, and documentation. If anyone is in danger or injured, contact emergency services first.

Your current situation:

- **Incident:** Collision-related — `INC-TSL-20260708-0427`
- **Vehicle:** Not drivable
- **Emergency response:** Resolved
- **Tesla Insurance:** Active
- **Recovery status:** Not started/completed; transport handoff is still unresolved

### What to do next

Use **Tesla Roadside Assistance immediately**, rather than starting a new insurance claim. In the Tesla app:

1. Open the app.
2. Select **Roadside** at the bottom of the home screen.
3. Choose the collision/vehicle condition that applies.
4. Confirm your location and vehicle details.
5. Select **Request help**.

Tell Roadside Assistance that the vehicle is **not drivable after collision** and provide incident reference **`INC-TSL-20260708-0427`**. Tesla’s official guidance distinguishes immediate towing from the insurance-claim process: Tesla Insurance customers needing a tow should use **Roadside Assistance**.

After the vehicle is secured, use **Tesla Insurance → Claims** in the app to manage the collision claim, repair facility, estimates, and documentation. If anyone is in danger or injured, contact emergency services first.
